big_picture: The government cut special additional excise duty on petrol by Rs 10 per litre to shield consumers from rising crude prices amid the West Asia crisis. Union Minister of State for Finance stated that the government has taken steps to counter the impact of the West Asia crisis. Parliament passed the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026, with Prime Minister Narendra Modi calling it a boost to ease of doing business. Both Houses cleared six bills, including legislation to make Amaravati the sole capital of Andhra Pradesh and to fix the quota for IPS officers in higher CAPF posts. The Rajasthan High Court warned that the Transgender Persons Amendment Bill risks turning gender identity rights into entitlements decided by the state. Union Minister Sukanta Majumdar declared that the BJP’s West Bengal CM will be non-vegetarian, responding to Mamata Banerjee’s meat ban claim. The draft IT amendments expand state power by making advisories binding and widening content takedowns, risking over-censorship and surveillance.

story_clusters.headline: Govt has taken steps to counter West Asia crisis impact: MoS Finance
story_clusters.summary: The Indian government has implemented measures to mitigate the economic impact of the West Asia crisis, as reported by the Ministry of Finance. Meanwhile, India and Saudi Arabia have engaged in high-level discussions regarding the conflict, with a second call between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman since the war began. Additionally, an EY report warns that the conflict could reduce India’s FY27 GDP growth forecast by 1 percentage point.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has imposed restrictions on banks offering rupee non-deliverable derivatives (NDFs) to both residents and non-residents, disrupting the $149 billion-a-day offshore rupee market. The move, aimed at curbing currency speculation amid the rupee's decline, has been supported by the Centre and aligns with efforts to stabilize the market. The restrictions have also widened the dollar-rupee forwards-NDF spread.

Reports indicate that delays in LPG delivery—reducing wait times from 40 days to 2 days in some states—have triggered an exodus of gig workers, with one company, NeuroPixel, shutting down. Additionally, Tamil Nadu’s textile sector is reportedly missing 3 lakh migrant workers due to LPG-related disruptions.

story_clusters.summary: The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) has exempted investments made before FY18 from the General Anti-Avoidance Rule (GAAR) following concerns raised by Tiger Global's case. This amendment aims to restore tax certainty for legacy investments, as reported by multiple sources.

Finance.summary:
Economists expect the Reserve Bank of India to maintain the repo rate unchanged due to persistent inflation risks and a neutral stance.

The Reserve Bank of India issued a Master Direction – Facility for Exchange of Notes and Coins under Section 35A of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, addressed to all banks.

The Reserve Bank of India tightened foreign exchange rules by banning non-deliverable forward contracts.
Finance.top_article_title: RBI may keep repo rate unchanged as inflation risks persist

Governance.summary: The IFSCA mandates a certification course for Key Managerial Personnel (KMPs) in intermediaries to standardize regulatory knowledge and improve governance. India’s CSR framework, now a statutory obligation under Section 135, faces legal and accountability challenges in its role as a parallel mechanism for local governance. The Assembly Election prompted the ECI to appoint Sandeep Rai Rathore as the head of Tamil Nadu police, alongside Additional Director General Sanjay Kumar as Commissioner of Police in Tambaram and Abhishek Dixit as Commissioner of Police in Madurai.
Governance.top_article_title: IFSCA Mandates Certification Course for KMPs to Improve Governance in Intermediaries

Civil Liberties.summary: The Parliament passed the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, which removes the right to self-determination for transgender persons. The RBI directed banks and financial institutions to immediately freeze accounts and deny financial access to individuals newly listed under UNSC terror sanctions. Jayna Kothari’s excerpt argues that the right to self-determination is crucial for improving employment and education outcomes among transgender people in India.
Civil Liberties.top_article_title: The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026, and its Discontents

Politics.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) Bill with a voice vote, prompting Opposition members to protest and walk out. The Lok Sabha cleared a bill making Amaravati the sole capital of Andhra Pradesh, with the YSR Congress Party staging a walkout. The Lok Sabha passed a bill declaring Amaravati as Andhra Pradesh's capital, supported by the Telugu Desam Party, Bharatiya Janata Party, and Congress, while the YSR Congress Party opposed it.
Politics.top_article_title: Lok Sabha passes CAPF Bill with voice vote; Opposition members protest, walk out

Legal.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that blocking Input Tax Credit (ITC) without giving the taxpayer an opportunity to respond violates natural justice and that Rule 86A powers must follow due process. The Karnataka High Court dismissed a challenge by direct recruit District Judges to the seniority list and directed the state government to notify the cadre strength of judicial officers within one month.
Legal.top_article_title: ITC Cannot Be Blocked Without Hearing: Bombay High Court Ruling

Defence.summary: The Defence Ministry told a parliamentary panel that the DRDO is developing lethal autonomous weapons. A school strike in Iran’s Minab, reportedly hitting the Shajareh Tayyebeh school on February 28, killed more than 168 civilians. The military is advised to procure anti-drone systems and integrate them with legacy weapon systems to create an effective defensive umbrella.
Defence.top_article_title: DRDO is developing lethal autonomous weapons, Defence Ministry tells parliamentary panel

Energy.summary:
NTPC aims to achieve 149 GW capacity by 2032 by expanding renewable and nuclear projects in Uttar Pradesh, focusing on commissioning Singrauli Stage-III units and improving gas availability for Dadri and Auraiya plants to boost their Plant Load Factor.

The government has instructed refineries to redirect a portion of LPG feedstock to industries facing petrochemical shortages, including packaging and condom manufacturing, amid ongoing supply constraints.

Tata Power has resumed operations at its Mundra coal-fired power plant following new power purchase agreements, particularly with Gujarat, to support energy security in northern and western states.
Energy.top_article_title: NTPC eyes 149 GW by 2032, expands renewable and nuclear footprint in UP: Diwakar Kaushik

Foreign.summary:
Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov will visit Delhi in mid-May for a BRICS meeting.

Over 40 countries discussed "every possible diplomatic, economic and coordinated measure" to pressure Iran into reopening the Strait of Hormuz during a virtual summit hosted by UK Home Secretary Yvette Cooper.

The UN Security Council is set to vote on a Bahraini proposal authorizing "defensive means" to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while Donald Trump warned the US would target more Iranian infrastructure, including bridges and power plants.
Foreign.top_article_title: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov to visit Delhi mid May for BRICS meet

Labour.summary: The new labour codes provide a framework to strengthen wage-setting and worker protection, though implementation remains key. The labour codes seek to balance worker welfare and organisational efficiency by redefining employment categories under a unified legal structure. Workers in Panipat are protesting the four new labour codes amid economic hardships exacerbated by the US-Israel war on Iran.
Labour.top_article_title: Making the code on minimum wage work on the ground

Technology.summary:
Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah praised the passage of the Jan Vishwas Bill, 2026.

The Finance Ministry approved over ₹1 lakh crore for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0, with Cabinet approval expected by April-end.

NCP Leader Nasiruddin Patwary stated that Sheikh Hasina surrendered significant space to India and that the BNP government in Bangladesh appears to be following a similar path.
Technology.top_article_title: Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah hails passage of Jan Vishwas Bill, 2026

Trade.summary:
Australia will not raise drug prices despite Donald Trump’s 100% tariff on pharmaceuticals imported into the US, as Health Minister Mark Butler stated the federal government is not negotiating on price protections for common medications.

India agreed to extend the WTO e-commerce moratorium beyond its current expiration but opposed plurilateral agreements without safeguards, according to Commerce Minister New Delhi.

India and the US discussed next steps in Bilateral Trade Agreement (BTA) talks on the sidelines of the WTO Ministerial, with New Delhi awaiting clarity on Washington’s tariff framework before finalizing the agreement.
Trade.top_article_title: Australia says it won’t raise drug prices after Trump’s 100% tariff on pharmaceuticals imported into US

Welfare.summary: Parliament will hold a 3-day special session from April 16 to pass women's quota bills. The Punjab Cabinet approved the 'Mukh Mantri Mawan Dheeyan Satkar Yojna' providing ₹1,000 per month to women and ₹1,500 to Scheduled Castes women. The SMART project, supported by the World Bank and Palladium, helped tribal women in Ghoti mechanize rice processing, turning it into a year-round enterprise.
Welfare.top_article_title: Parliament 3-day special session from April 16 to pass women's quota bills

Urban.summary:
FY27 will introduce new real estate tax rules, including changes to capital gains tax and stamp duty, while simplifying compliance for homebuyers.

The Itanagar municipal body directed eateries to remove terms like ‘chicken’, ‘beef’, ‘pork’, and ‘mutton’ from their names, citing inappropriateness and inconsistency with public decency norms.

NBCC (India) secured a $75 million affordable housing project mandate in Seychelles, following recent domestic order growth and earnings improvement.
Urban.top_article_title: FY27 bring new real estate tax rules: What stays, what gets easier

Health.summary:
India’s anti-obesity drug market is expanding with patent expirations enabling low-cost generics from Indian firms, alongside upcoming new treatments and existing options like orlistat and liraglutide.

The government has permitted allocation of C3 and C4 categories for the pharma, chemicals, and food sectors.

Banks in India continue to overlook the rising debt risks linked to climate disasters such as heatwaves, pollution, heavy rains, floods, and other emergencies.
Health.top_article_title: Beyond Ozempic: A look at India’s anti-obesity drug market, what’s available now & what is to come

Infrastructure.summary:
The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) diverted all vehicular traffic from the old bridge to a newly constructed bridge across the Kabini River on the Mysuru-Nanjangud stretch of NH 766 for repairs to the deteriorating old structure.

The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) now permits residents to exchange Indian Rupees at forex counters located beyond immigration in airport departure areas to facilitate last-minute currency exchange for outbound travelers.

The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) exceeded its FY26 highway construction target by 15% through additional funding from internal resources, surpassing budgetary support.
Infrastructure.top_article_title: Bengaluru-Ooty highway: Traffic diverted from old bridge to new bridge across Kabini river on Mysuru-Nanjangud stretch of NH 766

Education.summary: NCERT has been granted deemed university status, enabling it to offer higher education courses and establish off-campus or offshore centers under UGC guidelines. NCERT director Dinesh Prasad Saklani announced that new NEP-aligned Class 9 textbooks will be released by April 15, with most already in print and set for rollout in CBSE schools starting the 2026–27 session. The Centre’s decision to grant NCERT deemed university status allows it to independently offer degree programs to strengthen teacher education and research.
Education.top_article_title: NCERT now ‘deemed to be university’; to offer higher education courses

Telecom.summary:
The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) released telecom subscription data as of February 2026.

India's telecom subscriber base reached 1.32 billion in February, with the sector adding 7.31 million subscribers at a growth rate of 0.56%.

Nokia’s report projects India’s 5G subscriber base to surpass 1 billion by 2031, noting average monthly mobile data consumption per user rose to over 31 GB in 2025 from 27.5 GB in 2024.
Telecom.top_article_title: TRAI releases Telecom Subscription Data as on February 2026

Agriculture.summary: A Stakeholders’ Meeting on the Climate Resilient Agriculture Programme was held on March 30, 2026, at the ICAR Research Complex for Eastern Region in Patna with participation from scientists, project workers, and farmers from Bihar’s Gaya and Buxar districts. India’s dairy sector leaders in New Delhi emphasized the need for a climate-resilient dairy ecosystem, highlighting integrated value chains, innovative financing, and scalable farmer-centric solutions. The Centre has taken action against fertiliser black-marketing and is reviewing MSP procurement.
Agriculture.top_article_title: Stakeholders Deliberate on Climate Resilient Agriculture at ICAR-RCER

Environment.summary:
53 gharials were released into Kuno National Park in Madhya Pradesh as part of conservation efforts for the species.

The Forest Department’s Kumki initiative in Chittoor has been effective in guiding wild elephant herds away from crop-raiding areas.

Governments remain divided over the timeline for the Intergovernmental Panel’s AR7 report, leaving the process in a deadlock.

Data Privacy.summary:
The Data Security Council of India appointed Neelam Dhawan as its new chairperson, succeeding Promod Bhasin, to enhance India's cybersecurity and data privacy efforts.

Neelam Dhawan will collaborate with industry and government stakeholders to strengthen cybersecurity measures and position India as a global leader in the field.

The USTR warned that rules under the DPDP Act may affect the operations of US credit bureaus in India.
Data Privacy.top_article_title: Nasscom's Data Security Council appoints Neelam Dhawan as chairperson

DeepTech.summary:
Sarvam AI is close to raising $300 million to $350 million at a $1.5 billion valuation to compete with AI leaders from the US and China.

Happiest Minds Technologies is preparing to launch its first agentic AI platform for engineering within weeks as its 18-month AI initiative transitions from strategy to product.

Anthropic’s AI coding tool, Claude Code, accidentally exposed its full source code due to a packaging error, though no user data or core AI systems were compromised.
DeepTech.top_article_title: AI startup Sarvam close to raising $300 million at $1.5 billion valuation

Fintech.summary:
UPI transactions in India rose 11% month-over-month to 22.64 billion in March.

UPI processed a record 22.64 billion transactions in March, up 24% year-over-year.

Bitcoin remained near the lower bound of its trading range amid uncertainty from the Middle East conflict.
Fintech.top_article_title: UPI Transactions Jump 11% MoM To 22.64 Bn In March

articles.title: Indicative Calendar of Market Borrowings by State Governments/ Union Territories for the Quarter April – June 2026
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has been guiding state governments and Union Territories with legislatures to adopt a Benchmark Issuance Strategy (BIS) for their market borrowings to finance fiscal deficits. The RBI, acting as the cash and debt manager, has been sensitizing states on this strategy to improve the issuance framework of State Government Securities and enhance secondary market liquidity. The article highlights the RBI's role in shaping fiscal policy through regulatory measures.
articles.raw_summary: Market borrowing by State Governments, including Union Territories with legislature (UTs), has become a major source of financing their fiscal deficit. Over the years, Reserve Bank has undertaken various measures to improve the issuance framework of State Government Securities and their secondary market liquidity. articles.title: IFSCA Mandates Certification Course for KMPs to Improve Governance in Intermediaries
articles.summary: The International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA) has issued a circular mandating a certification course for Key Managerial Personnel (KMPs) in financial intermediaries. The move aims to standardize regulatory knowledge, ensure compliance, and improve governance standards. This requirement applies to personnel in IFSCA-regulated entities, emphasizing the need for structured training to enhance operational and regulatory adherence.
articles.raw_summary: The circular addresses the need for standardized regulatory knowledge among intermediaries. It mandates certification for key personnel to ensure compliance and improve operational standards. articles.title: Bombay HC Quashes Reassessment for Invalid Section 151 Approval
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court quashed reassessment proceedings for a taxpayer because the approval was obtained from an incorrect authority, violating Section 151 of the Income Tax Act. The court clarified that reassessment sanctions must strictly adhere to the time limits and jurisdictional requirements under Section 151. This ruling strengthens safeguards against improper reassessment actions by tax authorities. The decision impacts how reassessment cases are handled under Indian tax law.

The ruling applies to customs and trade-related processes in India.

articles.title: Karnataka ends road tax exemption for electric cars: What it means for buyers

articles.title: Rate of Interest on Government of India Floating Rate Bond 2028
articles.summary: The rate of interest on Government of India Floating Rate Bond 2028 (FRB 2028) applicable for the half year April 04, 2026 to October 03, 2026 shall be 6.17 per cent per annum. It may be recalled that FRB 2028 carries a coupon, which has a base rate equivalent to the average of the Weighted Average Yield (WAY) of last three auctions (from the rate fixing day i.e., April 04, 2026) of 182 Day T-Bills, plus a fixed spread (0.64 per cent). Ajit Prasad           Deputy General Manager (Communications

articles.title: ITAT Quashes Reassessment for Missing Sec 143(2) Notice even after Section 148 Return
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a reassessment by tax authorities is invalid if a notice under Section 143(2) was not issued, even if a taxpayer filed a late return. The tribunal emphasized that the issuance of this notice is mandatory for reassessment validity. The ruling impacts tax administration and reassessment procedures in India.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT held that reassessment without issuing notice under Section 143(2) is invalid, even if return was filed late. The ruling emphasizes that issuance of notice is mandatory and absence of it makes the assessment void. The post ITAT Quashes Reassessment for Missing Sec 143(2) Notice even after Section 148 Return appeared first on TaxGuru .

The compliance is part of broader efforts to regulate digital platforms and protect user data.

articles.title: GST Raw Milk Exemption Dispute: From ASMT-10 to DRC-07
articles.summary: A dispute over GST tax exemption for raw milk arose because a taxpayer failed to respond to notices (ASMT-10), leading authorities to presume taxability despite the exemption. The case underscores the importance of timely replies and proper documentation to avoid unwarranted tax demands. The issue stems from procedural gaps in GST compliance rather than a policy change itself.
articles.raw_summary: The case shows that failure to respond to GST notices led to tax demand even for exempt supplies like raw milk. Authorities presumed taxability due to lack of evidence. articles.summary: Iran is reportedly drafting a protocol to monitor ship traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, a key maritime route for global oil trade, in collaboration with Oman. The move is framed as a response to wartime conditions. The strait is a critical passage for India's oil imports from the Middle East, making this development relevant to India's energy security and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: Iran is reportedly drafting a protocol to monitor ship traffic in the closed Strait of Hormuz with Oman, citing wartime conditions.

articles.title: Suspicion Cannot Replace Proof in Labour Charges: ITAT Deleted Ad-Hoc Disallowance
articles.summary: The ITAT (Income Tax Appellate Tribunal) ruled that suspicion alone cannot justify additions to labour expenses without concrete evidence. The tribunal deleted an ad-hoc disallowance, stating that proper documentation exists. This decision has implications for businesses and individuals dealing with labour expenses.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that ad hoc disallowance of labour expenses without concrete evidence is unsustainable. It ruled that suspicion alone cannot justify additions when proper documentation exists. The post Suspicion Cannot Replace Proof in Labour Charges: ITAT Deleted Ad-Hoc Disallowance appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Will the FCRA Bill row impact poll outcome
articles.summary: The FCRA Amendment Bill 2026, which allows the central government to seize assets of foreign-funded NGOs, has sparked protests in Kerala over fears it targets minorities. The bill was put on hold ahead of the 2026 Kerala Assembly Elections. The protests highlight concerns about civil liberties and minority rights, while the bill's status reflects ongoing political and governance discussions.
articles.raw_summary: FCRA Amendment Bill 2026 lets Centre seize foreign funded NGO assets, sparks protests in Kerala over minority targeting fears, bill put on hold ahead of polls.

articles.title: DIR-3 KYC Overhaul by MCA: From Annual Filing to a 3-Year Compliance Cycle
articles.summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) has overhauled the DIR-3 KYC compliance process for directors in India. Previously requiring annual filings, the new rule shifts to a 3-year cycle while imposing stricter update obligations to maintain accuracy. This reform aims to reduce repetitive compliance burdens while ensuring directors' details remain current. The change is part of the MCA's ongoing efforts to streamline corporate governance requirements.
articles.raw_summary: The reform addresses repetitive annual filing requirements for directors. The new rule reduces frequency while maintaining accuracy through stricter update obligations. The post DIR-3 KYC Overhaul by MCA: From Annual Filing to a 3-Year Compliance Cycle appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Upheld 200% Penalty for Bogus Political Donation Under Section 80GGC
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) upheld a 200% penalty for a bogus political donation claimed under Section 80GGC of the Income Tax Act. The case involved whether withdrawing the claim after detection counts as voluntary. The tribunal ruled that post-detection withdrawal is not voluntary, and the penalty for misreporting was correctly imposed. The ruling reinforces compliance with tax laws related to political donations.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether penalty applies when a bogus donation claim is withdrawn after detection. The Tribunal held that post-detection withdrawal is not voluntary, and penalty for misreporting was rightly imposed. The post ITAT Upheld 200% Penalty for Bogus Political Donation Under Section 80GGC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Reassessment Invalid as AO Failed to Issue 143(2) Notice After Belated Return
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a belated income tax return filed under Section 148 remains valid. However, the reassessment initiated by the Assessing Officer (AO) was declared void because the mandatory notice under Section 143(2) was not issued. This decision underscores the procedural safeguards in tax reassessment and highlights the consequences of non-compliance with legal requirements.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT held that a return filed under section 148 remains valid even if delayed. Failure to issue mandatory notice under section 143(2) makes reassessment void. The post Reassessment Invalid as AO Failed to Issue 143(2) Notice After Belated Return appeared first on TaxGuru .

The decision follows pushback from airlines, which raised concerns about the policy. The government has paused the order pending further review.

articles.summary: The ITAT tribunal found a clerical error in a tax case, which led to the double counting of short-term capital gains. articles.title: Supreme Court restores criminal proceeding over unauthorised sale of CSI Church land
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has restored criminal proceedings against the unauthorized sale of land belonging to the Church of South India (CSI). The land, held in trust for community benefit, saw irregularities in its alienation, raising public concern. The court's decision underscores the legal and public interest dimensions of trust property management. The case highlights the need for accountability in the administration of such properties.
articles.raw_summary: The land in question, though administered by a corporate body, is essentially trust property held for the benefit of the community, and any irregularity in its alienation is a matter of legitimate public concern. articles.title: GST Cancellation Set Aside as SCN Lacked name or designation of proper officer
articles.summary: The Supreme Court set aside the cancellation of a taxpayer's GST registration because the show-cause notice (SCN) failed to specify the name or designation of the proper officer. The Court held that this defect violated principles of natural justice. The ruling underscores the importance of procedural compliance in tax administration and could influence future GST enforcement practices.

articles.summary: The government is amending GST rules to change the 'place of supply' for intermediary services to the recipient's location starting March 30, 2026. articles.title: Governor Bound By Council Of Ministers' Advice On Remission & Premature Release Of Convicts: Madras High Court Full Bench
articles.summary: A full bench of the Madras High Court has held that the Governor is bound by the decision of the Council of Ministers in matters relating to the remission and premature release of a convict prisoner. Answering a reference made to it, the bench of Justice AD Jagadish Chandira , Justice GK Ilanthiraiyan and Justice Sunder Mohan held that the governor is bound by the decision of the council of ministers, whether he likes it or not and under no circumstances can the Governor take a different view. “

articles.title: Global leaders work to ease oil price surge as Trump signals more weeks of Iran war
articles.summary: Global leaders in New York and London are attempting to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route, amid ongoing military strikes between the U.S., Israel, and Iran. President Trump has indicated the conflict could persist for two to three more weeks. The surge in oil prices and regional instability may impact India's energy imports and economic policies, given its heavy reliance on crude oil from the Middle East.
articles.raw_summary: In New York and London, European and Middle Eastern countries tried to develop a plan to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, which has largely been closed since the war in Iran began. Following President Trump's address predicting two to three more weeks of war, the U.S. and Israel continued to bomb Iran, and Iran continued its strikes on Gulf countries and Israel. Nick Schifrin reports.

articles.title: CSR Reassessment & 80G Denial Based Only on Audit Objection Invalid: Bombay HC
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that reassessing corporate social responsibility (CSR) compliance or denying tax benefits under Section 80G based only on audit objections is invalid. The court emphasized that previously examined issues cannot be reopened without new tangible evidence, setting a precedent that restricts the government's power to reassess solely due to audit objections. This decision impacts tax policies and corporate governance, reinforcing legal boundaries on administrative actions.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that reassessment based solely on an audit objection is invalid as it constitutes a change of opinion. It emphasized that previously examined issues cannot be reopened without new tangible material. The ruling reinforces limits on reassessment powers. The post CSR Reassessment   80G Denial Based Only on Audit Objection Invalid: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

This decision impacts tax reassessment procedures and legal interpretations for future cases.

The decision highlights the need for robust evidence in tax assessments and may influence future tax policy and enforcement practices.

articles.title: No Tax on Property Deals Within 10% Gap: ITAT Allows Safe Harbour on DVO Value
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a 10% safe harbour applies to property valuations by the District Valuation Officer (DVO), not just stamp duty values. This means minor valuation differences within 10% cannot trigger tax additions under section 56(2)(x) of the Income Tax Act. The decision provides clarity on tax treatment for property transactions where valuations are close but not identical.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal held that the 10% safe harbour applies even to DVO valuations, not just stamp duty values. Minor valuation differences cannot trigger tax additions under section 56(2)(x). The post No Tax on Property Deals Within 10% Gap: ITAT Allows Safe Harbour on DVO Value appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that show cause notices (SCNs) can be challenged in writ jurisdiction under Article 226 of the Constitution in exceptional cases where fundamental legal flaws could lead to manifest injustice. The Court clarified that while courts generally do not interfere at the notice stage, judicial review is permissible if the SCN is vitiated by serious legal infirmities. This ruling impacts how administrative actions and notices may be contested in higher courts.

The Court observed that judicial review under Article 226 of the Constitution can be invoked where the show cause notice is vitiated by fundamental legal infirmities that could result in manifest injustice. articles.title: Agricultural Land Capital Gain Addition Deleted in absence of development or trading intent
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that gains from the sale of agricultural land are not taxable if there is no intent to develop or trade the land. The tribunal held that the land met conditions under Section 2(14)(iii) of the Income Tax Act, making the capital gains non-taxable. The case clarified that such sales do not constitute business income without development or trading intent.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved denial of exemption on sale of agricultural land. The Tribunal held that the land met conditions of Section 2(14)(iii), making gains non-taxable. The case examined whether land sale constituted business income. The Tribunal ruled that absence of development or trading intent invalidated such classification. The post Agricultural Land Capital Gain Addition Deleted in absence of development or trading intent appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The High Court of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh has held that a second FIR is not barred in law where it relates to a distinct and wider conspiracy, even if it is connected to an earlier incident, reiterating that the governing principle is the “test of sameness.” The Court was hearing petitions challenging an order of the trial court framing charges in a case arising out of an FIR, where one of the principal grounds raised was that the FIR was impermissible as a second FIR in view of an earlier FI

articles.title: LPG rumours: Centre urges remaining states to prevent panic buying after only 17 conduct briefings
articles.summary: The central government has urged states to intensify communication efforts to counter rumors about LPG supply shortages, which have led to panic buying. Only 17 states currently hold press briefings, and authorities are being asked to use regular press conferences and social media to reassure citizens about LPG availability. The goal is to prevent hoarding and black marketing of LPG cylinders.

Only 17 states currently hold press briefings. Authorities are asked to intensify proactive, regular communication via press conferences and social media to reassure citizens about LPG availability and distribution, while also curbing hoarding and black marketing.

articles.title: Real GDP growth may slow on costly crude: Credit rating agencies
articles.summary: Credit rating agencies Icra, Care Ratings, and Crisil have projected India's GDP growth at around 6.5% in FY27, citing high crude oil prices as a key factor. Icra assumes an average crude price above $85 per barrel, while Care Ratings uses $100 per barrel. Crisil notes that strong corporate balance sheets may mitigate credit risks despite these challenges.
articles.raw_summary: Icra projected GDP growth at 6.5% in FY27, assuming average crude price remains in excess of $85 a barrel this year, while Care Ratings projected a similar growth on higher average fuel-price assumptions-at $100 per barrel. However, Crisil expects robust balance sheet structures to provide sufficient undergirding against credit risks for an overwhelming percentage of industries it covers.

articles.title: The Third Islamic Republic

Applying this principle, the Court refused to interfere with the maintenance awarded to two minor child

articles.title: Securing the Skies: MoD’s drone vulnerability framework marks shift to secure warfare systems
articles.summary: The Ministry of Defence (MoD) has introduced a new framework to secure drone procurement by mandating vulnerability testing, secure firmware protocols, and supply chain audits. This initiative aims to shift defence systems toward cyber-resilient, secure-by-design standards. The move addresses growing concerns over drone vulnerabilities in military operations. The framework is part of broader efforts to enhance India's defence preparedness against emerging cyber threats.

articles.title: Govt hikes commercial LPG price amid global surge, shields households; regular petrol, diesel rates same
articles.summary: The Indian government increased the price of commercial LPG cylinders (19-kg) due to global supply disruptions and a 44% rise in Saudi benchmark rates, while shielding households by keeping domestic LPG and auto fuel rates unchanged. The Centre also capped the hike in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices despite losses faced by oil marketing companies (OMCs). The decision aims to balance global market pressures with domestic affordability.

articles.title: Pakistan fuel crisis deepens: Diesel up 55%, petrol rises 42% amid Hormuz disruption
articles.summary: Pakistan has raised petrol and diesel prices by 42% and 55% respectively in recent weeks due to instability in the Middle East disrupting global oil markets. The Pakistani government attributed the hikes to unsustainable fuel subsidies and called the increases unavoidable. The crisis highlights broader regional energy instability that could impact neighboring countries like India.
articles.raw_summary: Pakistan's recent surge in petrol and diesel prices, marking the second rise in just a few weeks, stems from the chaos of war in the Middle East that has shaken the stability of global oil markets. The government has described these increases as unavoidable to alleviate the mounting pressures of fuel subsidies on national finances.

articles.title: Oil prices today: Crude jumps as Trump dashes hopes of quick Iran war end; Brent tops $111
articles.summary: Middle East tensions escalated after US strikes on Iran, causing oil prices to surge. Brent crude jumped 7.8% to $109.03 per barrel, with supply concerns over the Strait of Hormuz pushing energy prices to multi-year highs. This could impact India's energy imports, inflation, and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: Middle East tensions escalated Friday, sending oil prices soaring as US strikes on Iran intensified. Brent crude climbed 7.8% to $109.03 per barrel, with WTI futures reaching record premiums. Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil transit route, are fueling supply concerns and pushing energy prices to multi-year highs.

The reforms are set to be implemented in phases, with full rollout by 2026.

Data localization rules and high tariffs on agriculture products are also cited. The US is pushing for more access to India's dairy market. Discussions for an interim trade agreement are ongoing.
articles.raw_summary: America has identified several trade barriers in India. These include India's preference for local satellites for DTH services and the exclusion of US payment firms from UPI. Data localization rules and high tariffs on agriculture products are also cited. The US is pushing for more access to India's dairy market. Discussions for an interim trade agreement are ongoing.

articles.title: Govt plans rare earth magnet push; pre-bid meeting set for 7 April
articles.summary: The Indian government is advancing a scheme to promote rare earth magnets, with a pre-bid meeting scheduled for 7 April. The meeting will clarify guidelines, eligibility, and implementation details for domestic and international industry players. This initiative aims to boost domestic production and reduce reliance on imports. The scheme falls under the government's broader push for self-reliance in critical technologies.
articles.raw_summary: The pre-bid meeting will provide the stakeholders, including domestic and international industry players and prospective bidders, an opportunity to gain a clear understanding of the scheme’s guidelines, eligibility criteria and implementation framework.

articles.title: The new Trump doctrine: Every country for itself
articles.summary: The article examines how the Trump administration's new doctrine of 'every country for itself' is weakening the global security framework that has maintained relative stability for decades. This shift, centered around American power, could disrupt international alliances and collective security arrangements, potentially impacting India's strategic and diplomatic decisions. The analysis highlights the erosion of a system that has historically underpinned global stability.

articles.title: Non-quoting of DIN — From “Fatal Defect” to a “Curable Lapse”
articles.summary: The article examines the legal classification of the non-quoting of the Document Identification Number (DIN) in India, shifting from being considered a 'fatal defect' to a 'curable lapse'. Authored by S. Vasudevan, Snehal Ranjan Shukla, and Sanjhali Bajaj, it explores the implications of this change for procedural compliance in government and regulatory actions. The discussion highlights how this shift may impact legal and administrative practices in India.

articles.title: LPG shortage hits MSMEs, disrupts production: Industry voices

articles.raw_summary: KarthiKeyan Mounaguruswamy, President of Coimbatore District Small Industries Association (CODISSIA) and Sunil Chordia, Chairman CII National, MSME Council & CMD at Rajratan Global Wire highlight how LPG supply disruptions are triggering cascading production cuts across MSMEs, forcing units to scale down operations and absorb higher fuel costs, with limited alternatives and mounting pressure on profitability and output.

articles.title: SARFAESI | Borrowers' Redemption Right Not Lost By Auction Sale If Balance Consideration Was Paid After Timelimit : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a borrower’s right to redeem a mortgaged property is not lost if the auction purchaser pays the remaining balance after the deadline. The case involved the Securitisation and Reconstruction of Financial Assets and Enforcement of Security Interest (SARFAESI) Act, which governs debt recovery processes in India. The decision clarifies legal ambiguities around auction timelines and borrower protections.
articles.raw_summary: The Court allowed the borrower to redeem the property despite the issuance of sale certificate, as the balance was paid by the auction purchaser belatedly.

The Reserve Bank of India's currency controls backfired, signaling a loss of confidence. A rupee at 100 threatens to increase living costs, strain government finances, and impact aspirational spending, posing a harsh check on the nation's ambitions.

A rupee at 100 threatens to increase living costs, strain government finances, and impact aspirational spending, posing a harsh check on the nation's ambitions.

articles.title: Possible To Reform Murderer, But Cyber Criminal Should Not Be Spared : CJI Surya Kant
articles.summary: During the hearing of a cybercrime case, Chief Justice of India Surya Kant today commented that it maybe possible to release and reform a murderer, but cyber criminals must not be spared at all. Referring to the accused before the Court, who apparently had a history of cyber frauds, the CJI said that a cyber criminal like him should be kept in single member cell without possibility of use of any mobile phone, etc. "That's the only way to deal", the CJI remarked. A bench of CJI Surya Kant, Justic

articles.title: HAL imposes damages on GE Aerospace for delays in supplying Tejas Mk-1A engines
articles.summary: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) has imposed financial damages on GE Aerospace for delays in supplying 99 Tejas Mk-1A fighter jet engines, of which only six have been delivered so far. The engines were ordered in 2021 for $716 million. The dispute highlights challenges in India's defence procurement and foreign partnerships, with potential policy implications for future contracts and regulatory oversight.

articles.summary: A Delhi Court has recently acquitted nine individuals, including brother of AAP Councillor Tahir Hussain— Shah Alam, accused in a case concerning violence and vandalism during the 2020 North-East Delhi riots. It claims opposition parties have united against corruption and intimidation in state politics. The remarks suggest systemic issues in governance and leadership selection, potentially undermining democratic processes.

People of Assam will no longer accept as leaders those involved in illegal activities and the mafia. Himanta Biswa Sarma only likes those who are connected to the stone, coal and areca nut mafia. Even in his party, those leaders who serve the people do not stay with Himanta Biswa Sarma, and he cancels their tickets."

articles.title: Oil price jumps and markets slide after Trump warning to Iran
articles.summary: Oil prices surged by 7% after US President Donald Trump warned of severe action against Iran, reversing earlier hopes of de-escalation in the Middle East. Brent crude crossed $108.50 per barrel, raising concerns about energy supply disruptions and their impact on global markets. The development could affect India's oil imports, fuel prices, and economic stability, given its heavy reliance on crude imports.

articles.title: Iran fires on Israel and Gulf neighbors as Trump claims threat from Tehran nearly eliminated
articles.summary: Iran launched attacks on Gulf states and tightened control over the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting global oil supplies. Former U.S. President Trump claimed Iran's threat to regional stability has nearly ended. The situation raises concerns about energy security and regional conflicts that could affect India's oil imports and diplomatic relations.
articles.raw_summary: Iran's attacks on Gulf states along with its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz have disrupted the world's energy supplies with effects far beyond the Middle East. That has proved to be Iran's greatest strategic advantage in the war.

articles.title: Oil and gas prices won't immediately return to normal even if the Iran war ends, the EU warns
articles.summary: The European Union's energy commissioner warned that oil and gas prices in Europe, driven up by the ongoing Iran war, will not return to normal levels even if the conflict ends soon. This situation could impact global energy markets and supply chains, including India's energy imports and pricing. The warning underscores the volatility in energy markets stemming from geopolitical tensions.
articles.raw_summary: The European Union's energy commissioner says skyrocketing oil and gas prices in Europe as a result of the ongoing Iran war won't return to normal levels any time soon, even if peace is declared tomorrow.

articles.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?

This situation could have significant implications for India's energy imports and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: The Iran war could end soon or drag on for much longer. Whenever the Strait of Hormuz is reopened, the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back.

articles.title: Penguin Random House Sues OpenAI Over AI ‘Memorisation’

The lawsuit highlights broader concerns about AI’s use of copyrighted material.

articles.title: Bihar plans 3 highway airstrips for emergency landings of Rafale, Sukhoi, Hercules
articles.summary: Bihar's state government is planning to build three highway airstrips to enable emergency landings of military aircraft like Rafale, Sukhoi, and Hercules. This move is motivated by recent conflicts in West Asia, where airbases have been targeted in wars. The airstrips aim to enhance India's defence preparedness and emergency response capabilities.
articles.raw_summary: Recent conflicts in West Asia, especially tensions involving the US, Iran and Israel, have shown that airbases are among the first targets in war.

articles.title: SEZ units can sell locally at lower duty for one year
articles.summary: India has announced a one-time relief for Special Economic Zone (SEZ) units, allowing them to sell goods in the domestic market at a lower customs duty for one year. This move may have implications for India's finance and infrastructure sectors.

articles.title: No public injury, no case: HC junks PIL against EC transfers of govt officials in poll-bound Bengal
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court dismissed a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) challenging the Election Commission's transfers of government officials in West Bengal ahead of elections. A division bench ruled that the petitioner failed to demonstrate that the transfers would cause administrative collapse and acknowledged the EC's authority in its own petition. The court found no public injury justifying judicial intervention in the EC's administrative decisions.
articles.raw_summary: Division bench ruled petitioner failed to show transfers would cause 'administrative collapse' — and had acknowledged EC's authority in its own petition.

articles.title: Hormuz crisis chokes shipping, sends freight rates soaring fivefold

This has caused freight rates to surge fivefold, impacting global trade and energy supply chains. India, heavily reliant on oil imports via this route, faces potential economic and energy security risks. The situation may prompt policy actions to mitigate disruptions in trade and energy flows.

articles.title: Omar Abdullah demands probe into Ganderbal encounter, after family claims ‘slain had no militant links’
articles.summary: Former Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah has called for a transparent and time-bound investigation into a security encounter in Ganderbal, after the family of a slain individual claimed the person had no militant links. Abdullah emphasized that dismissing the family's claim without scrutiny could harm public credibility and urged immediate action. The demand for a probe reflects broader concerns about accountability in security operations and civil liberties in conflict zones.
articles.raw_summary: “I believe the claim of the family should not be dismissed out of hand. At the very least this encounter needs a transparent and time bound probe with the facts made public. articles.title: The putrid mix of money and medical malpractice
articles.summary: A woman’s complaint about unpaid money for egg harvesting led Maharashtra police to uncover an illegal medical business network operating across three states. Seven people have been arrested, and investigators seized forged documents and hormonal injections. The case raises concerns about unregulated medical practices and potential exploitation in healthcare services.

articles.title: Kumkis help tackle a jumbo task in Chittoor
articles.summary: The Forest Department’s Kumki initiative in Chittoor uses trained elephants and mahouts to guide wild herds away from farmlands, reducing human-wildlife conflict. Conventional methods like firecrackers and vehicles proved ineffective, making this initiative a vital solution. The program involves daily routines, care, and bonding between elephants and mahouts. Author K. Umashanker explores the initiative’s impact and operations.
articles.raw_summary: The saga of wild elephants raiding crops along forest fringes had been a frequent news story. Conventional responses—firecrackers, drums, or vehicles—soon proved ineffective. Then came the Forest Department’s Kumki initiative, which began playing a vital role in guiding wild herds back. Their camp life— bond with mahouts and kavadis, daily routines, strength, and care—captures the essence of this initiative, explores K. Umashanker

articles.summary: Rising tensions around the Strait of Hormuz are prompting Gulf nations to explore alternative oil export routes, such as pipelines, to avoid disruptions. India, heavily reliant on oil imports, may face indirect consequences from such geopolitical developments.

articles.title: STT hike on futures and options takes effect from today — Here's all you need to know
articles.summary: The Securities Transaction Tax (STT) on equity futures and options has been increased by SEBI, effective immediately. The STT on equity options premium rose from 0.1% to 0.15%, on equity futures from 0.02% to 0.05%, and on equity options exercise from 0.125% to 0.15%. This change aims to adjust tax rates on derivative trading, impacting investors and market participants.
articles.raw_summary: STT on equity options premium has been raised to 0.15% from 0.1% earlier. The STT on equity futures has been raised to 0.05% from 0.02% earlier, while the STT on equity options exercise has been raised to 0.15% from 0.125% earlier.

articles.title: UPI achieves nearly 23 billion transactions in March: DFS
articles.summary: The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) recorded 22.64 billion transactions in March, a 24% increase from the same period last year. The Department of Financial Services (DFS) highlighted this milestone as part of India's growing digital payment ecosystem. The surge underscores the expanding adoption of UPI, which is a key component of the government's push for a cashless economy and financial inclusion.

articles.title: India’s tech hiring opens FY27 on a cautious note with fresher roles under most pressure: Report
articles.summary: Entry-level openings, i.e. roles targeting candidates with up to two years of experience, are flat at 15,000 for the second consecutive month, but are down nearly 11% year on year, giving jitters to freshers, the report said. Fresher-friendly roles account for barely 14% of total demand, with mid-senior professionals continuing to corner the lion's share at 54%.

The textile value chain is facing significant challenges due to these cost pressures.

This situation is expected to firm up sugar prices.

The situation highlights potential risks to international trade and energy supplies, which may have implications for India's trade and energy policies.
articles.raw_summary: Small businesses say pandemic supply disruptions were worse, but warn a prolonged Iran war could begin to have a similar impact if it stretches on for months.

articles.title: Fact check: How much oil does US import via Strait of Hormuz? Trump's ‘almost no oil’ claim debunked
articles.summary: US Energy Information Administration (EIA) data shows that the US imports 0.5 million barrels of crude oil per day via the Strait of Hormuz, accounting for 7% of its crude imports. This contradicts former President Trump's claim that the US imports 'almost no oil' through this route. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical chokepoint for global oil trade, and disruptions here could impact global oil prices and supply chains, indirectly affecting India's energy security and import costs.
articles.raw_summary: EIA data shows US imports 0.5 million barrels of crude oil per day via Strait of Hormuz: 7% of crude imports, contradicting Trump’s claim of ‘almost no oil.’
